content.full_text:
David Pitman, Chief, Political
Reform Division, Secretary of
State's Office

Aug. 18, 1977

Technical Assistance and Analysis Division

Advice concerning filing requirements

This memorandum is to confirm my advice to you and Kevin Monroe
on August 8, 1977 concerning the filing requirements of Government
Code Sections 84204.1 and 84206.

You stated that two legislators and their controlled committees
who filed consolidated campaign statements in connection with the
April/May 1977 Los Angeles municipal elections did not file off-year
campaign statements in July 1977 with your office. Since Section
84206 provides that every candidate and committee that receives con-
tributions or makes expenditures during the specified periods must
file an off-year statement "unless the...committee or elected
officer is required to file campaign statements in connection with
any election or elections held with the periods," you asked whether
the legislators and their committees are exempt.

I advised that the legislators and their committees are required
to file off-year statements under Section 84206. Section 84204.1 pro-
vides that a committee must file an abbreviated statement in connection
with an election if 1) the election is not held on the same day as a
statewide primary or general election; 2) the committee is not con-
trolled by one of the candidates in the election; 3) the primary pur-
pose of the committee is not related to that particular election; and
4) the committee raises or spends $250 or more in connection with the
election. Under the facts you have given us, it appears that the two
legislators and their controlled committees should have filed abbre-
viated statements in connection with the Los Angeles municipal elec-
tions. Section 84204.1 also requires that the information contained in
the abbreviated statement be repeated in any subsequent statement filed.
As to the period covered by the subsequent statement, Section 82046
provides that the beginning date of the period is the day after the
closing date of the most recent statement which was required to be
filed and states specifically that reference to the "most recent state-
ment" does not mean any abbreviated statement required by Section
84204.1

We are aware that some committees who should be filing abbre-
viated statements are instead filing complete statements. We do not

David Pitman                          -2-                    August 18, 1977

consider this to be a material violation of the Act since full re-
porting occurs in either case. But if a committee that should have
filed an abbreviated statement files a complete statement, it is
still required to comply with Section 84206 and file an off-year
statement for that period.

    Since our staff only recently clarified the filing require-
ments of Section 84204.1, we recommend that in the present instance
the legislators and their committees not be assessed late filing pen-
alties under Section 91013


                                        Nan Emmerson
                                        Special Compliance
                                        Representative

NE/gt

State of California

Memorandum

To    : Mickey Bennett
       Executive Director
       Fair Political Practices Commission

Date: June 17, 1977

From : Secretary of State

Subject: Lobbyist Activity Authorization - Form 61A

Attached is a copy of the existing Form 61A of which I have
a very limited supply at this time. Before I reorder more
forms, I would like to add the information as noted on the
attached.

The name and phone number would be of great help to us in
that we collect this information for public dissemination.

As I recall, from Bob Stern's remark of some time ago, the
reason there is no verification on the existing form is only
through oversight. If a verification is desired on this
form, please have it drafted and returned to me so that I
may order the forms not later than the 23rd of June.

WILLIAM N. DURLEY
Assistant to the Secretary of State
Elections and Political Reform

[signature]

DAVID B. PITMAN, Manager
Political Reform Division

DBP:tf

Attachment

Form 6TA
LOBBYIST

LOBBYIST ACTIVITY

AUTHORIZATION

(GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 86101)

I hereby authorize _________________________ to engage in the activities of a
(NAME OF LOBBYIST)

_________________________ 
(ADDRESS)

LOBBYIST as defined in Title 9, Chapter 2, Section 82039 of the California Government Code on behalf of

_________________________ _________________________
(NAME OF EMPLOYER) (ADDRESS)

for _________________________
(PERIOD OF AUTHORIZATION, IF KNOWN)

VERIFICATION:

Executed on _________________________ at _________________________
(DATE) (CITY AND STATE)

by _________________________
(SIGNATURE)

NAME _________________________ 
(TYPE OR PRINT)

Title _________________________ 
(RELATIONSHIP TO EMPLOYER)

PHONE NUMBER _________________________

sections.question: Whether two legislators and their controlled committees who filed consolidated campaign statements in connection with the April/May 1977 Los Angeles municipal elections are exempt from filing off-year campaign statements in July 1977 under Government Code Sections 84206 and 84204.1.
sections.conclusion: The legislators and their committees are required to file off-year statements under Section 84206. They should have filed abbreviated statements under Section 84204.1 in connection with the Los Angeles municipal elections. If a committee files a complete statement instead of an abbreviated statement, it is still required to comply with Section 84206 and file an off-year statement for that period. The staff recommends that these legislators and their committees not be assessed late filing penalties given that the filing requirements of Section 84204.1 were only recently clarified.
sections.question_synthetic: Are legislators and their controlled committees who filed consolidated campaign statements in connection with local municipal elections exempt from filing subsequent off-year campaign statements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advises that the legislators and their controlled committees are required to file both abbreviated statements for the municipal election and off-year statements under Section 84206, and recommends waiving late filing penalties due to recent clarification of the filing requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two separate items: primary advice letter (main content) and secondary memorandum about Form 51A. Some OCR quality issues but core question and conclusion sections are clear. Dated August 18, 1977 with signature from Nan Ematson, Special Compliance Representative.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether two legislators and their controlled committees who filed consolidated campaign statements in connection with the April/May 1977 Los Angeles municipal elections are exempt from filing off-year campaign statements in July 1977 under Government Code Sections 84206 and 84204.1.

CONCLUSION: The legislators and their committees are required to file off-year statements under Section 84206. They should have filed abbreviated statements under Section 84204.1 in connection with the Los Angeles municipal elections. If a committee files a complete statement instead of an abbreviated statement, it is still required to comply with Section 84206 and file an off-year statement for that period. The staff recommends that these legislators and their committees not be assessed late filing penalties given that the filing requirements of Section 84204.1 were only recently clarified.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to David Pitman's query regarding filing requirements for legislators and their controlled committees under Government Code Sections 84206 and 84204.1, concluding that off-year statements are required despite prior consolidated municipal election filings. The document also includes a separate memorandum regarding Form 51A (Lobbyist Activity Authorization) revisions.
